definition: The Kalecki fiscal buffer operates on aggregate corporate profits, not individual firm cash flows. Sufficient for IG/upper HY but insufficient for CCC/leveraged loans. A B-rated borrower at 8x true leverage refinancing at 400bp higher does not benefit from the aggregate demand floor — its interest coverage collapses regardless.
evidence: Kalecki identity operates at aggregate level, maturity wall operates at firm level
B-rated borrower at 8x true leverage refinancing at 400bp higher has collapsing interest coverage regardless of aggregate demand
Creates a bifurcated credit quality distribution wider than in prior cycles
Debate confirms this as a precise and correct critique of aggregate Kalecki thesis
open_questions: What is the threshold above which the Kalecki floor becomes irrelevant?
What share of the leveraged loan universe is above that threshold?
How wide is the credit quality bifurcation relative to prior cycles?
